data.body: Starting S2 impl. Read S1 (evidence.py/harness.py) + spike2 E2/E3 + README. Key constraint found: schema reference.kind enum is currently {import,symbol,dependency}; ev.validate() rejects kind=file/member on an attached reference. Design decision: validate_reference() is my own 5-value closed-set validator at the INPUT boundary; on the OUTPUT record I attach the reference field only when its kind is in the schema's *currently-valid* enum (read at runtime from grounding.schema.json), else I carry the ref identity in detail and leave reference=None. So every emitted record passes ev.validate() today, and auto-upgrades to always-attach once the integration patch extends the enum. Building resolve.py now (ctags -R --output-format=json index, ambiguity+member guard per E2, .rebar/grounding.toml optlib slot, deterministic import/from extractor).
